source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Minor Head Injury
source.population: Adults and children with minor head injury; red-flag, anticoagulated, non-accidental, or deteriorating presentations require escalation.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth minor-head-injury assessment, warning signs, imaging context, advice, and escalation; concussion diagnosis and local imaging decisions remain clinician-led.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports mechanism and time, loss of consciousness, headache, nausea or vomiting, dizziness, blurred vision, amnesia or confusion, medication and bleeding-risk review, vital signs, Glasgow Coma Scale, pupils, neurological screen, scalp, and neck documentation.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician-documented observation, head-injury advice, analgesia decisions, worsening-symptom return precautions, and arranged follow-up without fixed routine timing or doses.

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports mechanism and time, loss of consciousness, headache, nausea or vomiting, dizziness, blurred vision, amnesia or confusion, medication and bleeding-risk review, vital signs, Glasgow Coma Scale, pupils, neurological screen, scalp, and neck documentation.

items.rationale: Supports mechanism, symptoms, bleeding risk, neurological and neck documentation for child minor-head-injury follow-up; no decision is generated.

items.final_wording: Lists loss of consciousness, seizure, repeated vomiting, drowsiness, worsening headache, amnesia, focal neurological or balance or speech or vision changes, skull or penetrating injury, bleeding disorder or anticoagulant use, intoxication, and non-accidental injury concerns.

added_items.final_wording: Supports mechanism and time, loss of consciousness, headache, nausea or vomiting, dizziness, blurred vision, amnesia or confusion, medication and bleeding-risk review, vital signs, Glasgow Coma Scale, pupils, neurological screen, scalp, and neck documentation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Minor Head Injury
source.population: Adults and children with minor head injury; red-flag, anticoagulated, non-accidental, or deteriorating presentations require escalation.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth minor-head-injury assessment, warning signs, imaging context, advice, and escalation; concussion diagnosis and local imaging decisions remain clinician-led.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports mechanism and time, loss of consciousness, headache, nausea or vomiting, dizziness, blurred vision, amnesia or confusion, medication and bleeding-risk review, vital signs, Glasgow Coma Scale, pupils, neurological screen, scalp, and neck documentation.

added_items.rationale: Supports mechanism, symptoms, bleeding risk, neurological and neck documentation for child minor-head-injury follow-up; no decision is generated.

added_items.final_wording: Lists loss of consciousness, seizure, repeated vomiting, drowsiness, worsening headache, amnesia, focal neurological or balance or speech or vision changes, skull or penetrating injury, bleeding disorder or anticoagulant use, intoxication, and non-accidental injury concerns.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Minor Head Injury
source.population: Adults and children with minor head injury; red-flag, anticoagulated, non-accidental, or deteriorating presentations require escalation.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth minor-head-injury assessment, warning signs, imaging context, advice, and escalation; concussion diagnosis and local imaging decisions remain clinician-led.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists loss of consciousness, seizure, repeated vomiting, drowsiness, worsening headache, amnesia, focal neurological or balance or speech or vision changes, skull or penetrating injury, bleeding disorder or anticoagulant use, intoxication, and non-accidental injury concerns.

added_items.rationale: Supports deterioration and neurological warning context for child minor-head-injury follow-up; no decision is generated.

added_items.final_wording: Supports clinician-documented observation, head-injury advice, analgesia decisions, worsening-symptom return precautions, and arranged follow-up without fixed routine timing or doses.
